title: phlox gracilis subsp. humilis
intro: Phlox gracilis subsp. humilis, also known as low phlox or creeping phlox, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of China. Traditionally, Phlox gracilis subsp. humilis has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving pain and reducing fever, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
